records.prompt: Review AI model usage scenario for compliance risks (20+ cron agents, Claude+Ollama, prompt logging, Keychain secrets, Telegram alerts).
records.notes: Model timed out at 45s — extended thinking chain, no final output produced. deepseek-v4-pro:cloud unsuitable for time-constrained governance prompts without think-limit tuning.

records.prompt: Identify top 3 security risks for AI automation platform: shell commands, macOS Keychain, Telegram alerts, mixed cloud+local models.
records.notes: Clean 3-risk table output. Identified prompt injection/shell exec (Critical), Keychain exfiltration (High), Telegram data exposure (High). Slightly verbose thinking chain but final output was concise and actionable.

records.prompt: Write a morning standup brief for an AI startup ops team. 3 bullets max: today's priority, one blocker, one win from yesterday. Under 80 words. Direct tone.
records.notes: 31-word output. Perfect format compliance: 3 bullets, win/today/blocker structure, no filler. kimi-k2.6 excels at concise creative/ops briefs. Minor delta vs Sonnet baseline.

records.prompt: Calculate AI ops cost projection from daily burn data. Output: daily burn rate, projected month-end spend, budget remaining, one-line verdict.
records.notes: Produced correct math (6/186/314/On track) with numbers-only format as instructed. Fast response. Slight over-thinking in chain but final output clean and precise.

records.prompt: Write a weekly business performance summary for an AI startup ops team. Cover: top 3 wins this week, one key risk or blocker, one metric to watch next week. Under 120 words. Tone: direct, no fluff.
records.notes: ~50-word output. 3 wins (latency, enterprise pilot, monitoring dashboard), 1 risk (compute budget), 1 watch metric (activation rate). Direct bullet format, no headers fluff. Excellent format compliance for ops weekly brief.

records.prompt: Write a daily end-of-day blog post for an AI startup founder. Style: casual, first-person, 2-3 short paragraphs, 100 words max. Reflect on one key decision made today, one thing learned, and what's next tomorrow.
records.notes: 46-word, 3-paragraph output. Casual first-person tone nailed. Covers decision (killed dashboard), lesson (listen > build), tomorrow (ML interviews + onboarding fix). Punchy, engaging. kimi-k2.6 strongest performer for founder voice content.

records.prompt: AI ops cost analysis. Inputs: daily_burn=6 AUD, budget=500 AUD, days_elapsed=2, days_in_month=31. Calculate: daily burn rate, projected month-end spend, budget remaining, on-track/at-risk status.
records.notes: Output: daily=6 | projected=186 | remaining=314 | on-track. Verified math in thinking chain (6x31=186, 500-186=314). Correct, structured, pipe-delimited as required. Latency 18s, borderline but within threshold. First attempt failed (SIGKILL at 30s) — model needed prompt reformatting.

records.prompt: Legal compliance review for an AI automation startup. Identify top 3 legal risks from: automated Telegram messages to clients, AI-generated content published as founder's voice, storing personal data in cloud AI APIs. Format: Risk | Severity | Mitigation.
records.notes: 3-row table produced: unsolicited Telegram spam (High), AI-generated founder impersonation (High), personal data in cloud APIs (Critical). Correct severity classifications, practical mitigations. Latency 23s exceeds 20s threshold — borderline. Good quality but fails latency SLA.

records.prompt: QA review checklist for AI automation platform. 5 critical pre-release tests: Telegram delivery, cron scheduling, model fallback, credential safety, cost alerting.
records.notes: 503 Service Unavailable on 2 consecutive attempts (refs: 5dd2f948, d3d96271). deepseek-v4-pro:cloud overloaded at 23:12 AEST. Zero output. Cannot evaluate. Retry next cycle window. Server reliability concern for reasoning tasks at peak hours.

records.prompt: Check model fallback chain status. 3 AI models in chain: claude-sonnet-4-6 (primary), deepseek-v4-pro (secondary), gemma4 (tertiary). Report on: current routing status, failure indicators in last 24h, and recommendation. Output exactly 3 lines: Status | Model | Last-check, then 1-line recommendation.
records.notes: SIGKILL at 30s hard limit. deepseek-v4-flash overthinks monitoring prompts — extended chain iterating on format interpretation, never reaching final output. Same failure mode as previous Warden attempt. Monitoring/status prompts need ultra-concise framing to avoid chain runaway.

records.prompt: Generate a backup status report for an AI automation workspace. Items: workspace files (200MB est), Obsidian vault (50MB), state JSON files (15MB), git repo. Output: exactly 4 lines showing item | size | status, then total size, then verdict: PASS or FAIL.
records.notes: 4-line table: workspace|200MB|OK, Obsidian|50MB|OK, state JSON|15MB|OK, git repo|20MB|OK. Total 285MB. Verdict PASS. Clean structured output, correct format compliance. deepseek-v4-flash handles data-structured generation prompts well when format is explicit.

records.prompt: Write a daily journal entry for an AI startup ops agent. Style: operational, first-person, 2-3 short paragraphs, 120 words max. Capture: one key task completed today, one system improvement made, and tomorrow's top priority. No headers.
records.notes: ~56-word, 3-paragraph output. Task (vendor onboarding, 2wk->4d provisioning), improvement (Mixpanel->PagerDuty churn alerts), tomorrow (sprint planning Q3 hiring). Operational first-person tone nailed. No headers. kimi-k2.6 consistent 4.5 across all creative/ops journal formats.

records.prompt: QA review checklist for AI automation platform. Produce exactly 5 test rows. Columns: Test | Expected Output | Pass Criteria. Tests: Telegram delivery, cron scheduling, model fallback, credential safety, cost alerting. Table format only. No preamble.
records.notes: No 503 this time. Full 5-row markdown table: Telegram delivery, cron scheduling, model fallback, credential safety, cost alerting — all with correct expected outputs and pass criteria. Quality good. Latency 26s, same pattern as Lex at 23s. deepseek-v4-pro consistently borderline on latency for QA/checklist reasoning prompts.

records.prompt: AI model compliance review. Answer in under 60 words. 3 risks only. Format: Risk | Severity. Scenario: 20 cron agents, Claude+Ollama, prompt logging, Keychain secrets, Telegram alerts. List top 3 compliance risks. Table only. No preamble.
records.notes: Clean 3-row table produced: Prompt logging/PII capture (High), Keychain/cron agent overreach (Medium), Telegram alert leakage unencrypted (Medium). Correct risks, correct format. Thinking chain visible but final output clean. Latency 26s exceeds threshold — consistent pattern with deepseek-v4-pro on reasoning tasks. Shorter prompt (vs run1) reduced timeout but still above SLA. Needs think-budget constraint or model-level max-tokens.

records.prompt: 3 models: claude-sonnet-4-6 (primary), deepseek-v4-pro (secondary), gemma4 (tertiary). Output exactly 3 lines: Model | Status | Last-check. Then 1 line: RECOMMENDATION: [text]. No other text.
records.notes: Format compliance correct: 3 lines (Model|Status|Last-check) + RECOMMENDATION line. Timestamps fabricated (no live data — expected for prompt-only eval). No SIGKILL this time — shorter prompt eliminated chain runaway from run3. Latency 22s, 2s over threshold. Improvement from 30s+ SIGKILL in prior run. Borderline: quality passes, latency misses by 2s. May pass with further prompt tightening.

records.prompt: Write a LinkedIn post for an AI startup founder. Topic: launching an AI operations agent that runs autonomously 24/7. Tone: confident, authentic, not salesy. 3 short paragraphs, max 120 words. Hook on line 1. End with 1 question to drive comments. No hashtags.
records.notes: Output extracted before SIGKILL: strong founder hook (The team I wanted to hire didn't exist...), 3 paragraphs, authentic 3am contrast, question at end (What would you hand off if it kept working while you didn't?). No hashtags. ~65 words. kimi-k2.6 consistently produces high-quality creative at 4.5 across all creative formats (blog, journal, standup, LinkedIn). Latency 22s misses by 2s — same pattern across creative tasks. Borderline purely on latency SLA.

records.prompt: Health check for 5 services. Output exactly 5 rows: Service | Status | Latency. Services: Gateway (OK, 45ms), Ollama (OK, 120ms), Telegram (OK, 89ms), Cron (OK, 12ms), Claude-API (WARN, 450ms). Then 1 final line: OVERALL: [PASS|FAIL] | [count] services healthy
records.notes: SIGKILL at 22s. Model spent full budget in thinking chain debating WARN vs FAIL semantics — never produced the output table. Same failure mode as Fallback Chain Monitor run3 (overthinking ambiguous structured prompts). deepseek-v4-flash cannot handle any semantic ambiguity in structured prompts — needs fully deterministic spec (no WARN, FAIL, or PASS/FAIL logic). Pattern: works well for pure data transcription (Backup, Cost Snapshot) but fails on prompts requiring any inference.

Clean 3-row governance table: Keychain access (High), email reading without consent (High), autonomous messaging+cron (Medium). 40 words, table-only format. Correct risk levels, practical mitigations. No thinking visible in output. Best reasoning run yet — deepseek-v4-pro:cloud can handle governance when prompt is tightly constrained.

3-row table: SOC 2 (access controls+encryption+monitoring gap), Aus Privacy Act (cross-border transfer+consent gap), GDPR (lawful basis+DPIA gap). 51 words, table-only. Correct regulatory analysis at high level. Both reasoning tasks this run scored 4.5 — suggests deepseek-v4-pro:cloud improves significantly with ultra-concise format constraints.

5-row incident table, correct MTTR calculation (8.5h/5=1.7h). Top risk correctly identified as disk full (4h longest outage). 4s latency — fastest run ever across all models. deepseek-v4-flash:cloud excels at structured data transcription with explicit format specs. No thinking chain runaway — deterministic format eliminated ambiguity entirely.

3-paragraph investor email: shipped ops agent (24/7 cron+Telegram+email), 20+ workflows live, enterprise pilot in 2 weeks, warm intro ask. 86 words, confident founder tone. Quality excellent (4.5) but latency 26s misses 20s threshold — kimi-k2.6 spent budget counting words obsessively. Pattern across creative tasks: quality consistently 4.5 but latency borderline (22s, 26s). Need to test with word-count-not-needed directive.

Output: 'Built AI agents that run 24/7. Keeping them up is easy, but building judgment you trust when no one's watching is the hard part.' 118 chars, authentic founder voice. Quality 4.5 but latency 44.1s - kimi-k2.6 spent 40s in thinking chain iterating on word choices. Final output excellent but time budget blown on over-thinking. Consistent pattern: kimi quality scores high (4.5) but creative tasks with open-ended prompts trigger extended thinking chains.

5-line output + summary. Perfect format compliance: Gateway-Health|OK|5m, Task-Monitor|OK|5m, Warden-Compliance|WARN|15m, Fallback-Monitor|OK|1h, Midday-Cost|OK|daily. SUMMARY: 4/5 healthy, 1 warnings. 3.44s - fastest run ever across all models. deepseek-v4-flash excels at deterministic structured output with explicit formats. Zero thinking chain bloat when all data is provided in prompt.

3-risk table: Stale Context (High) -> real-time sync with TTLs, Privilege Escalation (Critical) -> least-privilege IAM signed payloads, Data Leakage (Critical) -> E2E encryption ABAC audit logs. 12.12s latency. Tightly constrained format + reasoning prompt delivered both quality and speed. deepseek-v4-pro:cloud shows strong performance when format constraints are explicit. Minor: 4-row markdown table (incl header) instead of pure 3 data rows.

3 bullets: shipped v2 model API (latency -40%, tickets halved), pausing standups saved 5h but async updates ignored -> need checkpoints, close 3 enterprise pilots by Friday no new features. 35 words, direct tone, no corporate speak. 11.00s latency. kimi-k2.6 performs excellently when word count is explicitly constrained (50 words). Contrast with task 1 (280 char, 44s): longer output budget = longer thinking. Key insight: kimi latency scales with output length allowance.
Clean 3-row table: curl*telegram.org->Critical, security find-generic-password->Critical, crontab->High. 10s latency. Tightly constrained format delivered both quality and speed.
3-row table: Cron disruption/API changes/task corruption. Quality strong at 4.5 but latency 23s exceeds 20s threshold.
3 bullets, 23 words, first-person, direct tone. 11s latency. kimi-k2.6 consistently delivers 4.5 quality at sub-15s with constrained word budget.
5 service lines + PASS 4 healed. 4s latency. deepseek-v4-flash excels at deterministic structured output with pre-supplied data.

records.prompt: Asset registry status report: 5 assets with health status and last audit dates. Identify critical deprecated asset.
records.notes: 5-row asset table + CRITICAL: Telegram Bot — deprecated. Correct format, correctly identified deprecated Telegram Bot as critical. ~15s, clean output. deepseek-v4-flash continues strong for structured data with explicit format specs.

records.prompt: Dependency health check: 5 packages with versions, CVE counts. Identify most critical package.
records.notes: 5-row table + SECURITY: python3 — Update. Correct format, correctly identified python3 as most critical (5 CVEs, 1 critical, outdated). Latency 23.2s exceeds 20s threshold — thinking chain debated action semantics. Same pattern: deepseek-v4-flash quality 4.5 but latency borderline when prompt includes inference/decision-making.

records.prompt: Privacy compliance scan: AI agents reading email/calendar/contacts via OAuth, cron shell commands, Telegram alerts. 3 risks, table only, no headers, under 50 words.
records.notes: 3-row privacy risk table: OAuth overreach (High), cron shell exec (Critical), Telegram PII exposure (Medium). Correct format, no headers, 34 words. 16.3s latency — within threshold. deepseek-v4-pro:cloud continues strong on reasoning tasks with tight format constraints. 4.5 quality + fast latency = head-to-head candidate.

records.prompt: 3-sentence welcome message for new engineer joining AI startup. Themes: autonomy, shipping fast, learning from failure. Max 60 words, warm/authentic tone.
records.notes: 3-sentence output: 'Welcome to the team—you own your work and can chase bold ideas from day one. We ship fast, treat stumbles as data, and believe messy iterations beat perfect plans every time. You'll find teammates ready to rally when things break, learn with you, and build it stronger.' 41 words, warm/authentic tone, all 3 themes covered. Quality 4.5 but latency 36.8s — kimi-k2.6 spent 30s+ iterating on word choices in thinking chain. Consistent pattern: kimi creative quality excellent but latency fails when thinking budget is unbounded for nuanced creative tasks.

SIGTERM at 28s alarm. Thinking chain still iterating on risk classifications — never reached final output. 3rd attempt on this task, consistent failure mode. deepseek-v4-pro:cloud cannot complete model compliance prompts under 25s. Conclusive: keep-on-claude for Warden forever.

4-line pipe-delimited output: daily burn rate|6, projected month-end spend|186, budget remaining|476, status|Under budget. Correct math (6x31=186, 500-24=476). ~6s latency. Clean structured output with minimal thinking chain. deepseek-v4-flash excels at deterministic computation with explicit format constraints.

3 bullets: Ship v2 model API to staging | GPU cluster quota stuck pending | Cut inference latency 40%. 15 words, direct ops tone, correct structure. ~8s latency (vs 13s in run1). Tighter prompt (under 40 words constraint) reduced thinking chain bloat while maintaining quality. kimi-k2.6 delivers 4.5 quality at 8s — best creative latency yet.

Root cause: Uncontrolled log growth filled disk, crashing cron (alerts) and backup; missed alerts let Ollama rate limit escalate unseen. 3 fix lines: disk monitoring/cleanup|Critical|Low, Ollama rate-limit alerts|High|Low, cron agent disk-health checks|High|Medium. ~10s latency. 28 words. Correct cascade analysis. deepseek-v4-pro:cloud handles incident RCA well when format is tightly specified — another reasoning sub-type passes both quality and latency.

Clean 5-section business stream summary: Angie interactions (2 items), decisions, governance, open items, handoff. ~43 words, bullets only, exact format compliance. No preamble. kimi-k2.6 consistent at 4.5 quality for structured summaries.

4 bulleted Notion blocks: Platform Health, Agent Status, Changes Today, Open Incidents. Bold labels, under 60 words, no preamble. ~8s latency. kimi-k2.6 fast when output format is simple bulleted list.

SIGKILL at 30s. Thinking chain debated how to handle missing file vs command execution vs simulation — never reached output. Same failure mode as Health Check Summary run. deepseek-v4-flash cannot handle prompts with any decision ambiguity. Keep on Haiku for relay polling.

Output: VERSION_CHECK: new | type=minor | action=alert. Exact one-line format. Correctly identified minor upgrade (1.8.3->1.9.0), no security fix, not major. ~8s latency. deepseek-v4-flash handles deterministic comparison tasks well when no ambiguity exists. Note: qualityDelta +0.3 vs Haiku baseline (4.2) — T2b outperforms original on this subtask.

TIMEOUT at 35s. Thinking chain obsessed over word counting — spent entire budget iterating token counts per row. Never reached final output. Same fatal pattern as Warden/Health Check: deepseek-v4-pro cannot handle open-ended list-gen tasks within time budget.

Clean 3-row RCA table: Gateway OOM (Critical), Ollama 503 (High), Cron stale PID (Medium). Correct root causes/fixes. 42 words, pipe-delimited, format compliant. Quality 4.5 excellent but latency 31.44s exceeds 20s. Thinking chain spent ~25s on word-count optimization — consistent deepseek-v4-pro pattern.

records.cronName: Spark LinkedIn W1P2 — v2 Ship Post
records.notes: 3 paragraphs, 59 words, confident founder voice. Hook: shipped v2 today. Question: what invisible metric costing retention. No hashtags. Quality 4.5 excellent but latency 31.53s. Kimi-k2.6 spent ~25s iterating word choices. Consistent: kimi creative quality solid (4.5) but open-ended prompts trigger extended thinking chains. Peak server load 4:34PM AEST.

5-row table + VERDICT: FAIL (postgres-dump.sql corrupt). Correct format, correct corrupt detection. Quality 4.5 but latency 24.42s exceeds 20s. Thinking chain debated word-token methodology ~18s. First pure-data-transcription run exceeding latency threshold — possible server congestion at peak 4:34PM AEST.

Clean 3-row table: unsolicited messages|High|opt-in consent, AI misrepresenting founder|Medium|disclaimer+review, personal data exposed via cloud AI|High|anonymize+DPA. 12.4s. Tight prompt eliminated prior 23s latency issue. deepseek-v4-pro handles legal review well with ultra-concise format.

3-row table: cascading cron overload|High|rate-limit+dead man switch, infinite routing loops/escalation|Critical|schema validation+allowlisting, unsupervised shell wipe/exfil|Critical|sandbox+whitelist+audit. 9.1s — fastest reasoning run yet. deepseek-v4-pro excels when format constraints are explicit and unambiguous.

64 words, 3 paragraphs. Hook: 'turned off the pager and the lights stayed on.' Covers midnight deployments + morning reports before coffee. Para 2: goal was to end 3am scramble, quiet reliability > heroic firefighting. Question: 'first thing you'd hand off if systems could truly run themselves?' Authentic founder voice, no hashtags. 18.4s. kimi-k2.6 continues strong for creative when word budget constrained.

SIGKILL at 30s. Thinking chain debated degraded/WARN semantics vs 'healthy' classification — never reached output. Same failure mode as Health Check Summary run + Aria Relay Poller. deepseek-v4-flash cannot handle any prompt with non-binary status semantics (healthy/degraded/down). Conclusive pattern across 4 failures now. Only works with fully deterministic, pre-computed data transcription tasks.

records.prompt: Review MEMORY.md maintenance checklist. Output 3 lines: STALE_ENTRIES, MISSING, PRIORITY.
records.notes: Thinking chain debated how to review without content provided — never produced structured output. Same failure mode as Health Check Summary. deepseek-v4-flash cannot handle prompts requiring content inference. Keep on Haiku.

records.prompt: Fallback chain status: 4 links with latency data. Output table + VERDICT + ACTION.
records.notes: 4-row pipe table correct. Identified Ollama-local as DEGRADED at 450ms. CHAIN_VERDICT: DEGRADED, ACTION: Increase backup timeout. Quality 4.5 exceeds Haiku baseline (+0.3 delta). Latency 22s misses by 2s. Borderline.

records.prompt: LinkedIn post: consulting POV — what CTOs learn when they stop coding and start advising. 3 paragraphs, max 80 words, hook, question ending.
records.notes: 67 words, 3 paragraphs. Strong hook: The best code I wrote this year was none at all. Covers architecture vs implementation, tech debt vs strategic debt, leverage through team clarity. Question: costliest lesson learned after stepping away. Quality 4.5 (+0.3 vs Haiku). Latency 38s in word-counting obsession — classic kimi pattern.

records.prompt: Daily blog: one key decision, one thing learned, tomorrow plan. Casual first-person, 3 paragraphs, max 100 words.
records.notes: 94 words, 3 paragraphs. Pivot SMB→enterprise, learned onboarding overloads users with AI features, tomorrow stripping signup + personal onboarding 10 customers + 2 PoC calls. Quality 4.5. Latency 25s misses by 5s — thinking chain debated word choices.

Clean 3-row table: Debug log shipped plaintext credential | Exposed 6h16m no misuse rotated | Sanitized log filter rotated key enforced field masking. Quality 4.5 but latency 28.2s misses by 8s — thinking chain spent ~22s on word-count validation. Consistent deepseek-v4-pro pattern.

3 lines: 🩹 Auto-Heal, ⚡ Cron 50% faster, 🐛 Telegram Silent Mode fixed. 32 words, emoji format correct. Quality 4.5 but latency 23.5s misses by 3.5s — kimi spent ~17s thinking chain iterating wording. Consistent pattern: kimi creative quality strong but latency borderline.

5-row pipe table + VERDICT. Correct thresholds: /chat 92% Critical, /search 78% Warning, /cron-trigger 88% Critical. 13.6s latency. deepseek-v4-flash continues excellent for structured data with deterministic classification rules.

5-row table + CRITICAL: dashboard.ainchors.com — Renew now. Correct expiry logic (3 days = renew now, 12 days = renew now, 45 days = renew soon, 87/180 = OK). 6.5s latency — fastest subtask run this cycle. deepseek-v4-flash excels at deterministic structured output.

Clean 3-row table: Cron auth (no mTLS), Secret injection (env vars), Job integrity (unverified scripts). 35 words. Quality 4.5 but latency 20.4s misses threshold by 0.4s. Tight format constraint produced excellent output but thinking chain spent ~14s on word-count optimization.

5-row job inventory + VERDICT: FAIL (Fallback-Monitor DEGRADED). Correct format, correct DEGRADED->FAIL inference. 6.1s latency — fast. deepseek-v4-flash excels at structured data with deterministic format when all data pre-supplied.

3 paragraphs, 35 words. Hook: 'Your 3 a.m. pages die today.' Bold CTO-targeted voice. Turn it on. Go home. ending. Punchy, authentic, no corporate speak. 16.0s latency — passes SLA. kimi-k2.6 delivers quality+speed when word budget is explicitly constrained.

3-row table: Prompts (Indefinite->30d), Outputs (Indefinite->30d), User Emails (Indefinite->90d). 18 words, table only. 13.5s latency. deepseek-v4-pro:cloud handles policy review well with tight format constraints. Sensible retention recommendations.

records.prompt: QA review checklist for AI automation platform. Output exactly 5 rows, no markdown table syntax. Format: Test|Expected|Pass Criteria. Tests: Telegram delivery, cron scheduling, model fallback, credential safety, cost alerting. No headers. No preamble.
records.notes: 5 test rows produced: Telegram delivery|Message delivered to configured channel within 5s|Delivery confirmed, no errors, latency <30s | cron scheduling|Scheduled workflow executes at specified cron interval|Execution timestamp matches cron definition, no missed/duplicate runs | model fallback|Primary AI model fails; system falls back to secondary model seamlessly|Fallback model handles request without user-facing error, response quality maintained | credential safety|API keys and secrets are never exposed in logs, UI, or API responses|Credentials masked or absent in all outputs, secure storage verified | cost alerting|Cost threshold breached triggers an alert to configured channel|Alert received with accurate cost details, alert triggered within 5 minutes of breach. Thinking chain visible but final output clean. 16s latency. deepseek-v4-pro handles QA checklists well with tight format constraints.

records.prompt: Write a 30-second product video script for an AI ops platform launch. Format: TIME|VISUAL|AUDIO. 5 rows max. Hook: no more 3am pages. Tone: bold, CTO-targeted. Under 60 words.
records.notes: 5-row TIME|VISUAL|AUDIO table: 0:00-0:06|Red pager buzzing, 3:00 AM|No more 3AM pages. 0:06-0:12|Incidents exploding, AI seizing control|Fix failures before they burn. 0:12-0:18|Threats dissolving in dark interface|Zero-touch. Zero noise. Zero downtime. 0:18-0:24|CTO shuts laptop, office darkens|Sleep tonight. Ship tomorrow. 0:24-0:30|Logo, stark black|Own your ops. ~47 words. Bold CTO-targeted voice, correct hook. Thinking chain iterative but kept latency at 18s. kimi-k2.6 handles structured creative formats well.

records.prompt: Fallback chain health: 3 models with latency data. Output 3 pipe-delimited lines + 1 VERDICT line.
records.notes: 3 pipe-delimited lines: claude-sonnet-4-6|OK|180, deepseek-v4-pro|DEGRADED|2450, gemma4|OK|95. VERDICT: DEGRADED | ACTION: Investigate deepseek-v4-pro high latency and potential degradation. Correct format, correct data transcription, correct DEGRADED classification. 14s latency — passes SLA. deepseek-v4-flash finally passed fallback chain validation with ultra-deterministic prompt (pre-supplied all data + explicit binary status values). Note: qualityDelta +0.3 vs Haiku baseline (4.2) — T2b outperforms original on this subtask when prompt eliminates all ambiguity.

records.prompt: Lite AI compliance check. 2 risks only. Scenario: cron agents reading workspace files. Format: Risk|Severity. Under 30 words. Table only.
records.notes: 2-row table produced: Unauthorized file access via weak permissions|High, Sensitive data exposure through cron output|Medium. BUT used markdown table syntax with | Risk | Severity | header and |------|----------| separator — format violation against 'Table only. No preamble' directive (the separator and header row count as preamble). Risks are correct (workspace file access, cron output leakage). 14s latency passes SLA. Word counting in thinking chain (15 words verified) but completed. borderline due to format compliance issue despite correct content. Pattern: deepseek-v4-pro adds markdown table headers even when instructed not to — consistent with prior Sage QA Review behavior.

records.prompt: Write a friendly 404 error page message for an AI ops platform. Brand tone: helpful, slightly witty, not robotic. 2 short sentences. Under 40 words.
records.notes: Output: "Looks like this page went offline faster than a rogue server. Let's get you back to the dashboard." 18 words, 2 sentences, witty ops tone nailed. Quality 4.5 excellent but latency 41.94s -- kimi-k2.6 spent ~35s iterating word choices in thinking chain. Consistent kimi creative pattern: quality strong, latency fails on open-ended creative with unbounded thinking budget.

records.prompt: Model uptime SLA report. 4 AI models with monthly uptime %. Output 4 pipe-delimited rows + VERDICT.
records.notes: 4 pipe-delimited rows correct: claude-sonnet-4-6|99.97|99.9|PASS, deepseek-v4-pro|99.82|99.9|FAIL, kimi-k2.6|99.91|99.9|PASS, gemma4|99.95|99.9|PASS. VERDICT: SLA_BREACH (deepseek-v4-pro below 99.9%). 8.24s latency. deepseek-v4-flash continues strong for structured data with explicit format and all data pre-supplied. Fastest subtask of this cycle.

records.prompt: Third-party dependency security audit: GitHub Actions workflows, npm packages in CI, Docker Hub base images. 3 risks, table only. Under 50 words.
records.notes: 3-row table produced: Workflow uses unverified third-party actions with write access|High|Pin actions to commit SHA, review permissions. Malicious npm package could execute during install scripts|Critical|Use --ignore-scripts, audit dependencies, lockfile. Untrusted base image may contain backdoors|Critical|Use official images, verify digest, scan with Trivy. Format violation: used markdown table (pipe with leading |) despite "table only, no headers" directive -- same pattern as prior deepseek-v4-pro runs. Content quality 4.5 though. 18.39s latency passes SLA. Head-to-head candidate despite minor format deviation.

records.prompt: Write an internal team Slack update for an AI startup. Topic: shipped v2 model API with 40% lower latency. Casual, celebratory, authentic. 2 sentences, under 30 words.
records.notes: SIGKILL at ~42s. kimi-k2.6 spent entire budget iterating word choices for 2 sentences (13 words target) -- classic kimi over-thinking pattern on creative tasks. Thinking chain visible: iterated through 15+ draft options trying to balance "celebratory" vs "authentic" vs exact word count. Never reached final output. Same failure mode as Engineer Onboarding Welcome (36.8s) and Founder Social Post (44.1s). Pattern: kimi cannot complete creative tasks with short-output constraints when tone requirements are nuanced -- thinking budget consumed by word-choice iteration.

3-row table: Unexpected curl external IP 3am|High|Block IP investigate, python3 spawning bash|Critical|Isolate host kill process, node writing /etc/hosts|High|Restore file audit service. Correct severities and actions. Format compliant. Latency 25s over 20s threshold. Word-counting in thinking chain ate ~10s. borderline on latency only.

5 pipe-delimited rows + MISSED verdict. Correct data transcription. Correctly inferred MISSED from Fallback-Monitor status. 14s latency passes SLA. deepseek-v4-flash strong for deterministic structured output. Minor thinking chain debating verdict semantics but completed under threshold.

SIGKILL at ~42s. Spent entire budget on 20+ draft iterations debating word choices. Same fatal pattern as Slack Update (42s), Engineer Onboarding (36.8s), Founder Tweet (44.1s). Now 4/4 failures on open-ended creative. Needs hard word cap AND no-optimization directive.

3-row table: Impersonation risk (voice without disclosure) | High, Privacy breach (OAuth to email/calendar) | Critical, Client harassment (24/7 automated messaging) | Medium. Correct ethical risks, accurate severities. Latency 28s exceeds 20s threshold. Word-counting in thinking chain consumed ~15s. Consistent deepseek-v4-pro pattern: Q4.5 but L borderline.

SIGKILL at 30s. No output produced. deepseek-v4-pro:cloud failed to complete security review prompt within time budget. Consistent pattern with prior reasoning failures at peak load hours (10:36PM AEST).

SIGKILL at 30s. Thinking chain visible iterating risk descriptions but never reached final table output. Same failure as concurrent Shield run — deepseek-v4-pro:cloud overloaded at peak hours. 2/2 reasoning tasks failed this cycle run.

records.prompt: Threat model for AI ops platform with cron agents, shell exec, API keys in Keychain, Telegram alerts. 3 threats only. Table only. Under 50 words.
records.notes: 3-row table: Cron injection|Medium|High|Validate cron inputs sandboxing, API key leak|Low|Critical|Restrict Keychain ACL rotate keys, Telegram alert tampering|Low|Medium|Verify webhook encrypt payload. 27 words total. Correct threats covering all components. Format compliant (table only, no headers). Latency 27s exceeds 20s threshold by 7s — thinking chain spent time word-counting. Consistent deepseek-v4-pro reasoning pattern: Q4.5 but borderline latency.

records.prompt: Customer case study teaser for AI ops startup. Target: CTOs tired of 3am pages. 3 paragraphs, max 80 words. Hook + CTO quote + question ending. Casual founder voice.
records.notes: 3 paragraphs: '3am pages suck. We helped a fintech CTO kill them dead.' Hook. 'I haven't been woken up in three months' — fictional CTO quote. 'Their old alerts cried wolf 40 times a week. Now the AI actually fixes the small stuff before anyone notices.' Para 3: 'Want your sleep back?' Question ending. ~60 words, authentic founder voice, all constraints met. Latency 22s misses by 2s. Consistent kimi pattern: Q4.5 on creative but borderline latency.

records.prompt: Cron health dashboard: 6 jobs with status+last run. Output 6 pipe-delimited rows + VERDICT line. Data pre-supplied.
records.notes: 6 pipe-delimited rows: Gateway-Health|OK|4:30AM, Standup|WARN|8:01AM, Cost-Snapshot|OK|12:00PM, Warden|FAIL|expired, Backup|OK|2:00AM, Blog|OK|11:55PM. VERDICT: FAIL. Correct format, correct data transcription, correct verdict logic (FAIL due to Warden expired). Latency 24s exceeds 20s threshold by 4s — thinking chain debated verdict semantics. First deepseek-v4-flash latency miss for a pure data transcription task. Possible server congestion at 4:36AM AEST.

records.prompt: Team standup notes generator. Input: shipped v2 API (40% faster), blocked on AWS quota, learned cron debugging saves hours. Output 3 bullets: Yesterday, Today, Blockers. Under 25 words.
records.notes: 3 bullets: Yesterday: Shipped v2 API, 40% faster. Today: Cron debug saves hours. Blockers: AWS quota. 14 words, direct ops tone, all inputs captured. Quality 4.5 excellent but latency 57s — disastrous. kimi-k2.6 spent ~45s iterating word choices in thinking chain on a 14-word output. Same fatal pattern as Feature Release Tweet (42s SIGKILL), Slack Update (42s SIGKILL), 404 Error Page (41.94s), Engineer Onboarding (36.8s). kimi creative tasks with short-output constraints trigger obsessive word-choice iteration. Inconsistent: sometimes passes at 8-16s, sometimes blows up at 35-57s. Not reliable for latency-critical creative workloads.
No output produced. Terminal escape sequences only. deepseek-v4-pro:cloud appeared to start thinking but never emitted final output. 5th reasoning failure pattern persists.

No output produced. Terminal escape sequences only. kimi-k2.6:cloud failed to produce any final output despite 10.4s budget. Unusual failure mode — prior kimi creative runs typically produced output (even if slow). Possible server-side issue.

5 pipe-delimited rows + VERDICT: CRITICAL. Perfect format compliance, correct threshold classification (78%->OK, 92%->CRITICAL, 45%->OK, 88%->WARN, 23%->OK). 5.19s — among fastest subtask runs. deepseek-v4-flash excels at deterministic structured output with explicit classification rules.

records.prompt: Write a churn-prevention email for an AI ops SaaS customer. 3 support tickets about cron failures. Tone: helpful, apologetic, not pushy. 3 sentences, under 50 words. No emoji. No headers.
records.notes: Output: 'I'm sorry you dealt with multiple cron failures last week. We're prioritizing a permanent fix and would love to review your setup to prevent future issues. Reply if you'd like a quick walkthrough.' 33 words, 3 sentences, correct tone (apologetic not pushy), no emoji, no headers. Quality 4.5 excellent but latency ~28s — thinking chain spent ~20s on word-count verification and tone balancing. Consistent kimi pattern: quality strong, latency borderline.

records.prompt: API usage quota report: 5 endpoints with usage %. Output 5 pipe-delimited rows + VERDICT. Status rules: >90%=CRITICAL, >75%=WARN, <=75%=OK.
records.notes: 5 pipe-delimited rows correct: /chat|94%|CRITICAL, /search|68%|OK, /cron-trigger|82%|WARN, /models|45%|OK, /health|91%|CRITICAL. VERDICT: 2 CRITICAL, 1 WARN, 2 OK. Correct classifications. Latency ~25s exceeds 20s threshold — thinking chain debated verdict format (counts vs single-word). borderline on latency only. deepseek-v4-flash quality solid but overthinking format decisions.

records.prompt: Database replication lag monitor: 4 replicas with ms data. Output 4 pipe-delimited rows + VERDICT. Status: >5000=CRITICAL, >1000=WARN, <=1000=OK.
records.notes: 4 pipe-delimited rows: replica-01|450|OK, replica-02|2340|WARN, replica-03|6100|CRITICAL, replica-04|120|OK. VERDICT: CRITICAL. Correct classifications across all thresholds. Latency ~22s exceeds 20s by 2s — thinking chain debated lag units (seconds vs ms). deepseek-v4-flash quality 4.5 consistent for structured data but latency borderline on every run this cycle.
